summary: Merge admissibility is deterministic, rollback-bound, lineage-bound, and re-crucible-bound. Merge cannot bypass the selection arena or jump directly into router authority.
merge_admissibility_rules: Parents must already be admissible through the bounded tournament and promotion chain.
Merge candidates must demonstrate complementary strengths rather than arbitrary aggregation.
Merge candidates must not overlap failure domains in a way that worsens safety or rollback risk.
Combined evidence must show no regression relative to both parents on governed measures.
Merge outputs must bind explicit lineage links to both parents.
Merge outputs must carry rollback plans before any promotion claim.
post_merge_rules: Merged outputs return to crucible and lifecycle intake before any future promotion claim.
Merged outputs do not inherit router eligibility automatically.
Merged outputs remain below router and lobe authority until later ordered steps are separately ratified.
claim_ceiling: R3 binds merge admissibility only. It does not authorize router candidate pool entry, router cutover, lobe authority, externality, comparative claims, or commercial activation.
